lever_summary: Dense bool AXIS reduction. eval_reduce_bitwise_axes' DType::Bool non-reduce-all branch was the last reduction path still decoding each element's multi-index (flat_to_multi_into + multi_to_out_flat) AND matching Literal::Bool per element — the pass66 out-index odometer was never applied here. Replaced with the shared OutIndexOdometer (removes the per-element decode) plus an as_bool_slice() dense fold (consumes pass80's dense Bool storage, 1 byte/elem, no enum match).

alien_graveyard.primitive: strength reduction of per-element address arithmetic (incremental index odometer) + dense SoA gather
alien_graveyard.mapping: finish applying the reduction odometer (pass66) to the bool axis branch and fold the contiguous bool slice; the dominant win is decode removal, with the dense bool slice on top

method: Same-worker-calibrated A/B: baseline = reduction.rs git-stashed to the committed per-element-decode bool axis loop; an unchanged calibration bench (full dense bool ReduceAnd, eval/reduce_and_64k_bool_vec) ran alongside in both runs to normalize cross-worker speed. speedup = (base_axis/base_calib) / (after_axis/after_calib).
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'reduce_and_256_axis1_bool_vec|reduce_and_64k_bool_vec' --save-baseline {axB|axA}
rejected_sub_levers: dense-bool comparison OUTPUT (Vec<bool> instead of Vec<Literal::Bool>): calibrated ~0.92x (neutral) — the comparison's bottleneck is the per-element int_cmp/i128 conversion, not the output write; reverted, not shipped.

dense-vs-Literal-with-odometer bool axis: 1.23x — this only isolates the data-model delta once the decode is already removed; the real lever is the decode removal (odometer), measured vs the committed baseline.

change.lever: eval_reduce_bitwise_axes DType::Bool non-reduce-all branch: drive OutIndexOdometer (no flat_to_multi decode); dense as_bool_slice() folds the contiguous bool slice, else the unchanged Literal::Bool match loop (now also odometer-driven). Removed the now-unused checked_strides binding in this branch.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: tensor.elements visited in ascending flat order; OutIndexOdometer reproduces multi_to_out_flat(flat_to_multi_into(flat)) exactly (same as pass66)
isomorphism.bool_op: unchanged: same bool_init seed and bool_op (ReduceAnd &&, ReduceOr ||) applied in the same order
isomorphism.errors: as_bool_slice() is Some only for Bool dense storage; Literal-backed/malformed Bool inputs run the unchanged Literal loop (still type-checks each element); output Bool tensor

validation.name: dense_bool_axis_reduce_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib --release reduction
validation.result: passed: 33 tests incl dense_bool_axis_reduce_bit_identical_to_literal_path (ReduceAnd/ReduceOr x all rank-3 axis subsets, dense vs Literal)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --release --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
validation.result: passed (release dodges shared-target proc-macro rmeta drift in debug)
